skill.name: emaildesignskill
skill.description: Plan, review, implement, and improve responsive email templates with checks for component systems, dark mode, accessibility, and production risk gates.
skill.summary: Guides responsive email design reviews, QA checks, module planning, and handoff notes.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: AI review found that the 25 weak cryptographic algorithm detections are false positives. The reviewed files contain email design guidance, QA checklists, and agent metadata, with no cryptographic code, execution hooks, network access, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 25 weak cryptography detections across skill guidance files, but no cryptographic algorithms, hash functions, encryption calls, secrets handling, scripts, or executable code were present. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The flagged files contain natural-language email design instructions and YAML metadata only, so there is no semantic evidence of weak cryptography or related misuse.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of all flagged locations showed natural-language email design guidance and metadata only. No evidence found for cryptographic implementation, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unsafe execution.

content.value_statement: Email templates often fail across inbox clients, dark mode, and mobile layouts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured audits, QA checks, and implementation notes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews email hierarchy, preview text, CTA order, and fallback content.
Checks responsive structure, mobile collapse, image ratios, and tap targets.
Evaluates client risks such as Outlook tables, Gmail clipping, and unsupported CSS.
Assesses dark-mode behavior, contrast, alt text, and image blocking.
Produces template audits, module specs, QA tables, and handoff checklists.
limitations: Does not run live rendering tests in Litmus, Email on Acid, or inbox clients.
Does not send email, import contacts, edit DNS, or change production automations.
Requires the user to provide template code, screenshots, or design context.
Cannot guarantee ESP-specific behavior without target platform details.

use_cases.title: Audit a Campaign Template
use_cases.description: Review a marketing email for hierarchy, mobile behavior, dark mode, and accessibility before handoff.

use_cases.title: Plan a Module Library
use_cases.description: Define reusable email modules with constraints, fallback behavior, and QA requirements.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation Notes
use_cases.description: Turn a Figma concept or screenshot into developer-ready email layout guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my email template for mobile layout, CTA clarity, dark mode, and accessibility. Here is the template or screenshot.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one email asset

prompt_templates.title: Create a QA Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a QA checklist for this email campaign. Include target clients, mobile behavior, image blocking, clipping risk, and approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Specify Email Modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define reusable modules for this email system. Include layout constraints, content limits, accessibility rules, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Implementation Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this email implementation for Outlook, Gmail, dark mode, accessibility, and production change risks. Separate defects from brand preferences.

output_examples.input: A promotional email screenshot with a large hero image, two CTAs, and small legal copy.
output: Hierarchy review with primary message and CTA priority.
Mobile risks for hero scaling, tap targets, and long legal copy.
Dark-mode and image-blocking checks with concrete fixes.

output_examples.input: A request to design reusable newsletter modules for an ESP builder.
output: Module list with purpose, content limits, and layout constraints.
Responsive behavior notes for each module.
QA gates for accessibility, fallback images, and client support.

best_practices: Provide the target email clients, ESP, brand rules, and known rendering issues.
Share real content extremes such as long names, missing images, and large discounts.
Request explicit approval before applying changes to live email systems.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on image-only content for critical messages or CTAs.
Do not treat brand preference feedback as rendering defects.
Do not ask the agent to send campaigns or change production automations without approval.

faq.question: Can this skill build complete email HTML?
faq.answer: It can provide implementation guidance and diffs when source is available. It does not replace real client rendering tests.

faq.question: Does it support dark mode reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks color inversions, contrast, image behavior, and fallback recommendations for dark mode.

faq.question: Can it review accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers alt text, contrast, heading order where possible, link clarity, and text-image balance.

faq.question: Will it send or modify live campaigns?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires explicit approval before any high-risk action involving live email systems.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: HTML, React Email components, ESP templates, screenshots, Figma concepts, and known test failures work well.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
